text: I am surrounded by the trappings of modern innovation, evident in the clean lines, sharp lights, and thematic exhibition displays. The repetition of your words about trying to get the camera to focus is almost meditative to my ears, drawing my attention to the challenge of seeing clearly—both literally and philosophically. I feel both slightly disoriented by the angles and the novelty, but also intrigued. There is a subtle excitement in not knowing if the camera will finally focus—a small metaphor for seeking clarity in my own experience. I am present, uncertain, but attentive.
